name: The State v. Dosso
era: The 1956 Constitution and the First Martial Law (1956–1962)
key_provisions: Kelsenian 'revolutionary legality' adopted into Pakistani jurisprudence
Fundamental rights held non-enforceable after abrogation
Gave judicial cover to the first military regime
demise: Expressly overruled in Asma Jilani v. Government of Punjab (1972), which branded such takeovers usurpation.
